text.title: Gcp Resource Hierarchy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gcp-resource-hierarchy methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Org-wide controls (IAM, billing, allowed locations) MUST be applied at the organisation node and inherited.
rule.rationale: Inheritance + override is the model; applying org-wide controls at folder/project level leads to drift.

rule.statement: Folder layout MUST be `Folder per (BU × env)` for organisations with >3 projects.
rule.rationale: BU × env folders cleanly separate billing, IAM, and policy without making the tree unmanageable.

rule.statement: Project IDs MUST follow `{env}-{bu}-{service}-{purpose}` and be ≤30 chars.
rule.rationale: Pre-baked naming convention makes inventory + scripts + IaC predictable.

rule.statement: Billing accounts MUST be assigned at folder level (not project level) so cost roll-ups follow the BU/env tree.
rule.rationale: Project-level billing fragments roll-ups and complicates FinOps.

rule.statement: Every project MUST live under a folder; root-level projects are forbidden in production organisations.
rule.rationale: Root-level projects bypass folder-inherited policies and become drift hotspots.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
